operating_state: Total revenue reached $25.5 billion in Q3 2023, up 6.3% from prior year, with six-month revenue of $50.3 billion representing 6.0% growth driven by broad-based performance across all segments. MedTech delivered exceptional growth significantly enhanced by the December 2022 Abiomed acquisition, with Electrophysiology and Interventional Solutions showing strong double-digit expansion. Pharmaceutical segment grew led by STELARA, TREMFYA, and DARZALEX offsetting REMICADE biosimilar erosion, while Consumer Health posted growth driven by increased global Cough/Cold/Flu incidences.
cost_structure: Gross margin remained stable at 67.8% versus 67.0% prior year, reflecting effective pricing strategies offsetting commodity inflation pressures. Cost of products sold improved to 32.2% from 33.0% despite intangible asset amortization including Abiomed-related charges. Operating leverage was evident with segment earnings before taxes increasing 17.0% driven by MedTech's earnings growth from Abiomed integration and favorable litigation settlements.
material_events: Kenvue completed its initial public offering on May 8, 2023, raising $4.2 billion in net proceeds while Johnson & Johnson retained 89.6% ownership with plans for a split-off exchange offer. The company established a $9 billion present value reserve for proposed talc settlement following LTL Management's second bankruptcy filing. Share repurchases totaled $3.9 billion for the six-month period while quarterly dividends of $1.19 per share were maintained.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency translation negatively impacted results by $896 million for the six-month period with 2.2% negative impact on worldwide sales, particularly affecting international operations. Volume-based procurement impacts in China affected multiple MedTech franchises. The company maintains forward foreign exchange contracts and cross currency interest rate swaps to manage currency exposures, while operations in Venezuela, Argentina, and Turkey are accounted for as highly inflationary economies.
forward_outlook: Management expects completion of the Kenvue exchange offer will result in deconsolidation and presentation of historical results as discontinued operations in future periods. STELARA faces patent expiration in September 2023 with biosimilar competition not expected until January 2025 based on settlement agreements. The company anticipates a $0.5 billion tax benefit in Q3 2023 from favorable IRS guidance on foreign tax credits.
uncertainty_profile: Primary uncertainties center on talc litigation resolution with the second LTL bankruptcy case dismissed in July 2023 and ongoing appeals processes alongside the established $9 billion settlement reserve. Extensive product liability litigation continues across ELMIRON retinal injury claims, TYLENOL autism allegations, and ZANTAC cancer-related indemnification demands. Patent infringement lawsuits remain active for numerous pharmaceutical products with ongoing ANDA challenges, while government investigations persist including FTC inquiries into REMICADE contracting practices.
